# Beudox HR Portal - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based frontend application built with TypeScript, serving as the user interface for a comprehensive Human Resources Management System. The application follows a component-driven architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Technology Stack
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build System**: Vite (fast development and optimized production builds)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens
- **Routing**: React Router for client-side navigation
- **Backend Integration**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + real-time subscriptions)
- **State Management**: React hooks and context (no external state libraries)
- **UI Components**: Custom component library built on Radix UI primitives
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for E2E tests

## Application Structure

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap and root rendering
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Main dashboard/home page component
- `src/App.tsx`: Root application component with routing setup

### Component Organization
The application uses a hierarchical component structure:

#### Layout Components (`src/components/layout/`)
- `AppLayout`: Main application shell with responsive sidebar
- `AppSidebar`: Navigation sidebar with collapsible menu
- `TopBar`: Header bar with user menu and notifications
- `UserMenu`: User profile dropdown with logout functionality
- `NotificationBell`: Real-time notification indicator

#### Feature Components
- **Attendance** (`src/components/attendance/`): Attendance tracking and reporting
- **Leave** (`src/components/leave/`): Leave request and approval workflows
- **Payroll** (`src/components/payroll/`): Salary calculations and payroll management
- **Employee Profile** (`src/components/employee-profile/`): Employee data management
- **Evaluations** (`src/components/evaluations/`): Performance review system
- **Projects** (`src/components/projects/`): Project and team management
- **Settings** (`src/components/settings/`): Administrative configuration
- **Finance** (`src/components/finance/`): Financial reporting and summaries

#### UI Components (`src/components/ui/`)
- Comprehensive design system with 60+ reusable components
- Built on Radix UI primitives for accessibility
- Consistent theming with CSS custom properties

### Key Architectural Patterns

#### Component Composition
- Heavy use of compound components (e.g., `Command` with `CommandInput`, `CommandList`, etc.)
- Props drilling minimized through context and custom hooks
- Consistent prop interfaces with TypeScript

#### Data Flow
- Direct Supabase client integration in components
- Real-time subscriptions for live data updates
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Error boundaries for graceful failure handling

#### State Management
- Local component state for UI concerns
- Custom hooks for shared logic (`useAuth`, `useToast`, etc.)
- Context providers for global state (authentication, notifications)

## Key Business Logic

### Authentication & Security
- Supabase Auth integration with JWT tokens
- Mandatory password change flow for new users
- Role-based access control throughout the application
- Secure API key management via environment variables

### Attendance System
- Complex attendance calculations in `AttendanceSummary` component
- Working day calculations excluding weekends and holidays
- Overtime tracking (regular and holiday overtime)
- Anomaly detection (frequent absences, incomplete records)
- Statistical analysis with metrics like attendance rate and punctuality

### Data Processing
- Date/time handling with proper timezone considerations
- Currency formatting for payroll calculations
- File upload handling for documents and attendance data
- PDF generation for reports and invoices

## Database Integration

### Supabase Schema
The application interacts with 39 database migrations defining:
- Employee management tables
- Attendance tracking
- Leave management
- Payroll processing
- Project management
- Audit logging
- Company settings

### Edge Functions
- Server-side business logic for complex operations
- PDF generation (`generate-invoice-pdf`)
- AI-powered attendance parsing (`parse-attendance-ai`)
- Automated notifications (`send-notification`)
- Employee lifecycle management (`invite-employee`, `deactivate-employee`)

## Development Workflow

### Build Configuration
- Vite configuration optimized for React and TypeScript
- Path aliases for clean imports (`@/components/*`)
- Environment variable handling for different deployment stages
- CSS processing with PostCSS and Tailwind

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for utility functions and hooks
- Component testing with React Testing Library
- E2E tests with Playwright for critical user flows
- Visual regression testing capabilities

### Code Quality
- TypeScript strict mode for type safety
- ESLint with React and accessibility rules
- Pre-commit hooks for code formatting
- Bundle analysis for performance optimization

## Performance Considerations

### Frontend Optimization
- Code splitting with dynamic imports
- Image optimization with proper sizing
- Lazy loading for heavy components
- Memoization of expensive calculations

### Data Efficiency
- Selective data fetching with proper filters
- Pagination for large datasets
- Real-time subscriptions only when needed
- Caching strategies for frequently accessed data

## Deployment & Environment

### Environment Variables
- Supabase configuration (URL, keys, project ID)
- Build-time environment detection
- Secure key management

### Build Process
- Production-optimized bundles
- Asset optimization and minification
- Source map generation for debugging
- Static asset handling

This architecture supports a scalable, maintainable HR system with modern web development practices and comprehensive feature coverage.
